rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LENA MAE RENFRO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues that remain unaddressed: 1) No original small estate affidavit re Esma Renfro signed by Deshawn Renfro's 5 children as direct successors of Esma Renfro (rather than as successors of Deshawn Renfro who predeceased Esma Renfro) is on file. 2) No original small estate affidavit re James Renfro signed by Belinda Renfro's 2 children as indirect successors of James Renfro (through Belinda Renfro's direct succession) is on file. 3) No original small estate affidavit re Belinda Renfro signed by Charles Faultry as one of the direct successors of Belinda Renfro is on file. 4) No original small estate affidavit re Destiny Walker signed by her successors is on file. The mandatory Judicial Council form DE-300 must be attached to the affidavit because Destiny Walker died after April 2022. Note: If the missing small estate affidavits cannot be filed, the shares of Esma Renfro and James Renfro (postdeceased beneficiaries) must be distributed to their "personal representative" (defined to include the public administrator of the county of their last residence). See Probate Code sections 58(a) and 11802(a). If the value of the estate of either postdeceased beneficiary does not exceed $50,000, the public administrator has authority to proceed with summary administration without court authorization and issue a written certification of such authority to the petitioner to receive distribution from this estate. See section 7660(a)(2). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AIKO ONODERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/04/2024. The following notes were e-mailed to counsel on 10/09/2024: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a waiver signed by the trustee of the devisee trust (who appears to be the only person entitled to notice of this petition). 3) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250? 4) The petition must be amended to allege a specific period for which a continuance of administration is sought. 5) Unless a waiver of account signed by the trustee of the devisee trust as the sole distributee of this estate can be obtained and filed, a second interim account must be filed. The second account period cannot exceed 3 years from 01/01/2015. See LRSF 14.34(A). Third and fourth interim accounts, with each account covering a period not to exceed 3 years, must be filed separately before a final account can be filed. 6) The first account identified 100% ownership of real property at 2401 Lake Street, San Francisco, as an asset of this estate and presumably reported 100% of income and expenses of that real property as receipts and disbursements of the estate, but the estate only owns 70.97% as so corrected after the first account was settled. Should the order on the first account be set aside under Probate Code section 7250(c) based on misrepresentation defined to include the omission of a material fact?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HIDEKI KRIS OKA
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/16/24 at Counsel's request. Notice of Hearing did not show the complete title of the pleading that was filed, and notice was mailed to two of the beneficiaries at addresses that differ from their addresses shown in the initial and earlier petitions. A personal representative is required by law to file the I&A within four months from the date Letters are issued to him. If not all assets are known, a Partial I&A should be filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA STAVROS KINGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/18/24. (No earlier availability.) (1) The only correct form of notice of hearing in a decedent's estate is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is used, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) Nephews Arthur Boudros and Robert Robert Kingas post-deceased. How did the administrator determine that their respective children are their successors-in-interest? [LRSF 14.35 G.] (5) The petition caption mentions extraordinary fees, but none appears to requested. (6) Paragraph 27 show assets on hand as of 8/21/24, but the accounting period ended 7/31/24. (7) Paragraph 27 shows $1,724,368.45 assets on hand as of 8/21/24, but paragraph 32 show $1,724,573.45. (8) Paragraph 34, item #5 shows the Bank of America checking account ending xx2537, but the only other Bank of America checking account number shown in the I&A ended xx7284. (9) "Various Investment Holdings" does not set forth which specific holding were community property. (10) If the administrator hired and paid a third party to prepare the accounting, that amount must be deducted from her statutory compensation. (11) Bond does NOT appear to have been sufficient, as alleged in paragraph 4. (12) The omnibus clause does not distinguish between separate an community property. (13) For which assets did the estate pay taxes? (14) What does the administrator propose to do about the $12,841.43 in liabilities? (15) Attorney costs for "certified copies and filing" must be itemized to show what was certified or filed and when. Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of the amended petition (or second amended petition), proof of service, and any new papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/6/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a probate referee HAS BEEN appointed. The values in the Corrected I&A should have been used for the assets on hand as of the date of death, not the values show in the I&A that was superseded by the Corrected I&A.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]Not providing courtesy copies to the examiner can delay review. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.])

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE PICHOTTO PROPERTY TRUST DATED DECEMBER 24,1993
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Accounting, Demand For Distribution, For Breach Of Trust, Return Of Trust Property, Financial Elder Abuse; Removal And Appointment Of Independednt Trustee; Removal And Appointment Of Independent Trustee, Imposition Of A Constructive Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues (see tentative rulings for previous hearings) that remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. Note: As of 07/01/2024, a copy of the proposed order must be served in a contested matter. A proof of service must be filed. See LRSF 14.7(A)(4). 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the notice of hearing form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) Please provide a copy of letters issued to John Begg as personal representative of the estate of Shawn Crane. If no personal representative has been appointed, the name and address of each heir and devisee must be provided by a verified allegation, and notice must be given to each of them. See Probate Code section 17203(c)(1). 4) No summons has been issued and served as to the elder abuse cause of action. 5) No declaration required by CCP section 377.32 has been filed by the petitioner to whom the right to prosecute Leona May's elder abuse cause of action is alleged to have passed under Welfare & Institutions Code section 15657.3(d). 6) If there is no personal representative of the estate of Shawn Crane, each devisee must be served in the manner required by section 851(a)(2) as to relief under section 850 against Shawn Crane's estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
